tasks.description: Fix selection ring CSS bug (Steps 1-2). In `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx` around line 352, replace `transition-all duration-200` with `transition-[opacity,transform] duration-200` so that box-shadow changes (ring-2 ring-sky-400 for selection, hover:shadow-md) are instant instead of animated. Remove redundant `rounded-lg` inside the isSelected conditional at line 353 (already on base classes). Then verify state flow stability: confirm `gallerySelectionMap` in `GallerySelectionContext.tsx` only changes on explicit selection actions, confirm `selectedGalleryIds` useMemo is stable, confirm `handleImageClick` in `GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx` doesn't have stale closure issues. Fix any state issues found.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated the gallery item container to use `transition-[opacity,transform]` and removed the redundant selected-state `rounded-lg` class. Tightened `GallerySelectionContext` so identical replace/append/clear actions preserve map identity, which keeps `selectedGalleryIds` stable across no-op updates. Reviewed `GenerationsPaneGallery` and did not find a stale-closure issue in `handleImageClick`; its callback dependencies already track both the modifier state and selection action. Verified with the full related frontend test files.

tasks.description: Portal AgentChat to document.body (Step 4). In `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx`, wrap the component's returned JSX in `createPortal(..., document.body)`. Import `createPortal` from `react-dom`. This lifts the three fixed-positioned elements (recording overlay ~line 272, floating bubble ~line 311, expanded panel ~line 329) out of EditorPaneTab's transform context. Add a `typeof document !== 'undefined'` guard before the portal call per repo convention (see `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx:87-94` for the pattern). React portals preserve the component tree context, so all hooks/contexts remain functional.
tasks.executor_notes: Wrapped AgentChat output in `createPortal(..., document.body)` and added the repo-standard `typeof document === 'undefined'` guard before the portal call. This lifts the fixed-position bubble, overlay, and expanded panel out of pane transform context so the viewport anchoring is consistent for unlocked and locked pane states. Verified with the full AgentChat-related frontend test run and direct source checks.

tasks.description: Consolidate duplicated edge utilities into shared module (Step 6). Create `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/utils.ts` with: `isRecord` (use stricter version from generation.ts:22-24 that excludes arrays), `asTrimmedString`, `asPositiveNumber`, `asStringArray`. Update consumers: (a) `tools/generation.ts` — remove local copies, import from `../utils.ts`. (b) `tools/create-task.ts` — remove local `asTrimmedString`, `asPositiveNumber`, `asStringArray`, import from `../utils.ts` (this file does NOT use isRecord). (c) `llm/messages.ts` — remove local `isRecord`, import from `../utils.ts`, AND re-export isRecord so the 4 downstream consumers (index.ts, db.ts, tool-calls.ts, selectedClips.ts) that import isRecord from `./llm/messages.ts` continue to work without changes. CRITICAL: messages.ts must re-export isRecord — `export { isRecord } from '../utils.ts'` — to preserve its export surface for those 4 files.
tasks.executor_notes: Created `utils.ts` with the stricter shared helpers, removed duplicate local helper implementations from `tools/generation.ts` and `tools/create-task.ts`, and moved `llm/messages.ts` onto the shared `isRecord` while preserving its public API with `export { isRecord } from '../utils.ts'`. Verified the four downstream imports in `index.ts`, `db.ts`, `tool-calls.ts`, and `selectedClips.ts` still point at `./llm/messages.ts`, and ran the ai-timeline-agent edge tests successfully.

tasks.description: Decompose loop.ts runAgentLoop function (Step 7). In `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.ts`, extract from the 212-line `runAgentLoop` (lines 45-257) into module-level functions in the same file: (1) `cleanAssistantText(text: string): string` — the 5 regex substitutions at lines 189-196. (2) `executeToolCall(toolCall, registry, supabase, timelineId, config): Promise<ToolResult>` — tool dispatch at lines 142-186 including parseError/run/create_task branching. (3) `buildToolErrorTurn(toolCallId: string, error: unknown): AgentTurn` — error recovery message at lines 202-209. The main function becomes orchestration-only: init → loop → invoke LLM → process tool calls → persist state.
tasks.executor_notes: Extracted `cleanAssistantText`, `executeToolCall`, and `buildToolErrorTurn` into module-level helpers and moved per-iteration tool processing behind `processToolCalls`, leaving `runAgentLoop` focused on initialization, loop control, LLM invocation, dispatch, and persistence. Added direct loop helper coverage and re-ran the full ai-timeline-agent edge test module successfully.
commands_run: rg -n "export function cleanAssistantText|export async function executeToolCall|export function buildToolErrorTurn|async function processToolCalls|export async function runAgentLoop" supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.ts

tasks.description: Refactor command-parser.ts to dispatch map (Step 8). In `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/command-parser.ts`, replace the 15 sequential if-statements in `parseCommand` (lines 45-189) with a command dispatch map: `const PARSERS: Record<string, CommandParser> = { view: parseViewCommand, ... }`. Handle aliases by mapping both keys to the same parser (e.g. delete/rm → parseDeleteCommand, generate/gen → parseGenerateCommand). The parseCommand body becomes: tokenize → normalize alias → lookup parser → call or throw unknown-command error. Each parser is a small named function in the same file.
tasks.executor_notes: Replaced the sequential command `if` chain with explicit parser functions, a `COMMAND_ALIASES` normalization map, and a `PARSERS` dispatch table. Added parser tests for canonical commands, alias handling, and the unknown-command path, then re-ran the full ai-timeline-agent edge test module successfully.
commands_run: rg -n "type CommandParser|const COMMAND_ALIASES|const PARSERS|export function parseCommand" supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/command-parser.ts

tasks.description: Decompose useExternalDrop.ts (Step 10). Extract duplicated track creation logic into a shared helper: `createTrackAtTop(dataRef, kind: 'audio' | 'visual', label?)` returning `{ trackId, insertAtTop: true }`. The `kind` parameter determines prefix (A* for audio, V* for visual) matching existing line 300 behavior. Extract per-drop-type handlers from onTimelineDrop (lines 177-473) into named module-level functions in the same file: handleTextToolDrop, handleEffectLayerDrop, handleFileDrop, handleMultiGenerationDrop, handleSingleGenerationDrop, handleAssetDrop. CRITICAL: preserve caller-specific semantics — handleMultiGenerationDrop passes trackId: undefined and insertAtTop per-item; handleFileDrop derives kind from file type; handleAssetDrop forwards trackId from drop target. The dispatcher in onTimelineDrop should become ~30-50 lines.
tasks.executor_notes: Refactored `useExternalDrop.ts` so `onTimelineDrop` is now a 25-line dispatcher, with module-level handlers for text-tool, effect-layer, file, multi-generation, single-generation, and asset drops. Consolidated track creation behind shared helpers, including `createTrackAtTop` for both audio and visual tracks, while preserving the original semantics: multi-generation drops still pass `trackId: undefined` when forcing new-track creation and asset drops still forward the resolved target track when reusing an existing track. Verified with the full related frontend test run plus direct dispatcher/handler checks.
commands_run:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts')
lines=p.read_text().splitlines()
start=end=None
for i,l in enumerate(lines,1):
    if 'const onTimelineDrop = useCallback(async' in l:
        start=i
        break
for i,l in enumerate(lines[start-1:],start):
    if '  }, [' in l:
        end=i
        break
print({'start':start,'end':end,'line_count':end-start+1})
PY

tasks.description: Minor cleanups (Step 11). (1) In `src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/hooks/useLassoSelection.ts:136`, replace inline `event.shiftKey || event.metaKey || event.ctrlKey` with a named helper `isMultiSelectEvent(event)` or import the logic pattern from useModifierKeys. (2) In `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChatMessage.tsx`, extract magic truncation numbers (80, 120) to named constants at module top (e.g. MAX_TOOL_NAME_LENGTH, MAX_ATTACHMENT_SUMMARY_LENGTH).
tasks.executor_notes: Replaced the inline modifier-key expression in `useLassoSelection` with `isMultiSelectEvent(event)` and extracted the AgentChatMessage truncation literals into `MAX_TOOL_NAME_LENGTH` and `MAX_ATTACHMENT_SUMMARY_LENGTH`. Verified the affected frontend files in the full related vitest run and with direct source checks.

tasks.description: Add ai-timeline-agent to edge vitest config and run full test suite (Steps 3, 5, 9, 12). (1) In `config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts`, add `'supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/**/*.test.ts'` to EDGE_UNIT_INCLUDE. (2) Run edge tests: `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts`. (3) Run all 10 frontend test files: MediaGalleryItem.behavior, GenerationsPaneGallery, dragDrop, useLassoSelection, useModifierKeys, MediaGalleryGrid, GallerySelectionContext, AgentChat, useAgentSession, useExternalDrop. (4) Run full `npx vitest run` as final gate. Fix any failures caused by the refactoring (update imports, function signatures). If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until all pass.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/**/*.test.ts` to `config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts`, then ran the required verification layers. The 10 named frontend test files passed cleanly: 10/10 files and 46/46 tests. In the official edge-config run, the `ai-timeline-agent` suites passed, but the overall edge run stayed red due to unrelated pre-existing failures in other edge-function areas. The final full `npx vitest run` gate also stayed red due to unrelated pre-existing failures outside the timeline-agent and gallery-selection changes; the refactored surfaces remained green in their targeted suites.

watch_items: isRecord re-export from messages.ts: When moving isRecord to utils.ts, messages.ts MUST re-export it (or all 4 downstream files — index.ts, db.ts, tool-calls.ts, selectedClips.ts — must be rewired). This is the #1 breakage risk in the entire plan.
CSS transition fix must use transition-[opacity,transform] not transition-all — the box-shadow property must NOT be transitioned, as both ring-* and shadow-* compile to box-shadow CSS variables
Portal typeof document guard: AgentChat portal must include `typeof document !== 'undefined'` guard per repo convention (DropIndicator.tsx:87-94)

useExternalDrop caller semantics: handleMultiGenerationDrop passes trackId=undefined intentionally for new-track creation; handleAssetDrop forwards trackId from drop target. These must be preserved exactly.
Edge function backward compatibility: no changes to session schema or tool call format. The utils extraction and loop/parser refactors are internal only.
useAgentSession.ts client-side copies of isRecord/normalizeTurns/isAgentSessionStatus are intentionally kept — do NOT try to share across client/server boundary in this pass
DEBT: agent-chat-rendering — ensure portal change doesn't affect how assistant-role turns render as chat bubbles
DEBT: agent-chat-undo-integration — agent edits arrive via query invalidation and bypass undo stack; portal doesn't change this but be aware
Run edge vitest immediately after T3 (utils consolidation) to catch import graph breaks early, don't wait for T4/T5

sense_checks.question: Does the selection ring now appear instantly (no flash/fade) when clicking a gallery item, and remain visible on hover? Verify transition-[opacity,transform] is the replacement and that no other transition-all exists on the same element.
sense_checks.executor_note: `MediaGalleryItem` now uses `transition-[opacity,transform]` on the selected container, the selection ring classes remain, and no `transition-all` remains on that element. The related frontend test files passed.

sense_checks.question: Does AgentChat render at bottom-5 right-5 relative to the viewport (not the pane container) in all three contexts: full editor page, unlocked pane, and locked pane? Is the typeof document guard present?
sense_checks.executor_note: `AgentChat` now portals its fixed-position UI to `document.body` and includes the `typeof document === 'undefined'` guard before calling `createPortal`, so its `bottom-5 right-5` positioning is viewport-relative instead of pane-relative.

sense_checks.question: After moving isRecord to utils.ts, does `llm/messages.ts` still export isRecord (via re-export)? Grep for all imports of isRecord from './llm/messages' and confirm index.ts, db.ts, tool-calls.ts, and selectedClips.ts are unbroken.
sense_checks.executor_note: `llm/messages.ts` now imports `isRecord` from `../utils.ts` and re-exports it. Verified `index.ts`, `db.ts`, `tool-calls.ts`, and `selectedClips.ts` still import from `./llm/messages.ts`, and ai-timeline-agent edge tests passed.

sense_checks.question: Is runAgentLoop now orchestration-only (init → loop → LLM → dispatch → persist)? Are cleanAssistantText, executeToolCall, and buildToolErrorTurn extracted as named functions with clear signatures?
sense_checks.executor_note: `runAgentLoop` now delegates assistant-text cleanup to `cleanAssistantText`, tool dispatch to `executeToolCall`, tool-specific fatal formatting to `buildToolErrorTurn`, and tool-call iteration to `processToolCalls`, so the main function reads as orchestration over init, loop control, LLM invocation, dispatch, and persistence. The ai-timeline-agent edge test module passed.

sense_checks.question: Does parseCommand now use a dispatch map? Are all 11+ command types covered including aliases (delete/rm, generate/gen)? Does the unknown-command error path still work?
sense_checks.executor_note: `parseCommand` now tokenizes once, normalizes aliases through `COMMAND_ALIASES`, dispatches through `PARSERS`, and preserves the unknown-command error path. The parser test file covers canonical command families and aliases including `rm`, `issues`, `gen`, and `settext`.

sense_checks.question: Is onTimelineDrop now under 50 lines as a dispatcher? Does createTrackAtTop handle both 'audio' and 'visual' kinds? Are the 6 per-type handlers preserving their original caller semantics (trackId=undefined for new tracks, insertAtTop forwarding)?
sense_checks.executor_note: `onTimelineDrop` is now a 25-line dispatcher. `createTrackAtTop` handles both `audio` and `visual`, and the extracted handlers preserve the caller semantics around `trackId=undefined`, `forceNewTrack`, and `insertAtTop` forwarding.

sense_checks.question: Is the inline modifier key check in useLassoSelection replaced? Are AgentChatMessage magic numbers (80, 120) now named constants?
sense_checks.executor_note: The lasso hook now uses `isMultiSelectEvent(event)` instead of the inline modifier-key expression, and `AgentChatMessage` now uses named constants for the previous `80` and `120` truncation values.

sense_checks.question: Do all 10 named frontend test files pass? Does edge vitest pass including ai-timeline-agent tests? Does full `npx vitest run` pass with zero failures?
sense_checks.executor_note: The 10 named frontend test files passed. The `ai-timeline-agent` tests also passed under the official edge vitest config after adding the permanent include entry. The overall edge-config run and the full `npx vitest run` gate did not reach zero failures because of unrelated pre-existing suites elsewhere in the repository; the final full-suite summary was 26 failed and 804 passed test files, with 53 failed and 4128 passed tests.

meta_commentary:
Execution guidance:

**Start T1 and T2 in parallel** — they touch completely different files (MediaGalleryItem.tsx vs AgentChat.tsx). T3, T6, and T7 are also independent of T1/T2 and each other — maximize parallelism.

**T3 is the riskiest task.** The isRecord re-export from messages.ts is the single most likely thing to break the build. The approach: create utils.ts with the canonical definitions, update generation.ts and create-task.ts to import from ../utils.ts, then in messages.ts replace the local isRecord definition with `import { isRecord } from '../utils.ts'` AND keep the `export { isRecord }` so downstream consumers are unaffected. Run edge tests immediately after T3, before starting T4/T5.

**T4 and T5 depend on T3** because they touch files in the same edge function directory and the edge tests should validate T3's import graph before further changes. However, T4 and T5 are independent of each other.

**Portal convention**: Check DropIndicator.tsx:87-94 for the exact typeof document guard pattern before implementing T2. The SSR guard is a repo convention, not optional.

**useExternalDrop (T6)**: The plan calls for 6 handler functions but read the actual code first — the drop types may have different names or there may be more/fewer. The key metric is reducing onTimelineDrop to a dispatcher. Don't over-abstract; keep handlers in the same file per user preference.

**command-parser (T5)**: Read the actual if-branches first. The plan says "15 if-statements" and "11 if-statement branches" in different places — count the real ones and build the dispatch map from what exists.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Fix CSS transition conflict in MediaGalleryItem.tsx
Step 2: Verify state flow stability in GallerySelectionContext/GenerationsPaneGallery/MediaGalleryGrid
Step 4: Portal AgentChat to document body
Step 5: Run AgentChat tests and verify
Step 6: Consolidate duplicated utilities into shared utils.ts
Step 8: Refactor command-parser.ts to dispatch map
Step 9: Add ai-timeline-agent to edge vitest config and verify
Step 12: Run full test suite (frontend + edge)

validation.completeness_notes: All 12 plan steps mapped. Steps 3, 5, 9, and 12 (all test/verification steps) consolidated into T8 as the final comprehensive test gate. Steps 1-2 consolidated into T1 since state flow verification is part of the same bug investigation.
